behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text. The behavior says "static prompt-template lint check," which is explicit local-only evidence. Rejected higher levels: not Integration/System because no module handoff, OS, network, or subprocess is required; not Agentic/Workflow because no model call is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with (a) a clean template and (b) a template containing a copied phase instruction snippet from SKILL.md; assert deterministic reject/accept results and precise violation location.

behaviors.description: Real harness-assembled prompt (after interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Leakage can be introduced at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture merge), so Unit is too low for this behavior’s failure mode. Rejected System via behavior text "after interpolation and fixtures" (local harness composition, no required real network/subprocess). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this checks prompt assembly content, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt builder pipeline with production-like fixtures and interpolation; assert final assembled prompt has no SKILL.md phase-instruction leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: Invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context (not hard-coded prompt rules).
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires validating behavior caused by skill context, not just wiring. A deterministic stub can verify loading, but cannot verify model behavior attribution. Rejected Workflow because this does not require multiple model decisions in sequence; single model response can establish the causal check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test through real harness with real model enabled, using user-style prompt and skill-file-provided context; assert observed behavior aligns with skill context while confirming prompt text itself does not embed hard-coded phase rules.

behaviors.description: Harness can run same user-style prompt against degraded/missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is harness configuration/file-loading indirection across modules (variant selection + prompt immutability). Rejected System: local filesystem variant swapping is sufficient; no required real HTTP/subprocess. Rejected Agentic: this behavior is capability to run variants with unchanged prompt text, not outcome quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire harness to two skill-context inputs (baseline vs degraded/missing), execute setup phase with identical prompt text, and assert only skill source changes while prompt text hash remains constant.

behaviors.description: If relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model output sensitivity to skill-content regression. Mocked model output would hide exactly this failure. Rejected Workflow because one modeled interaction is enough to detect regression; no mandatory multi-step agent pipeline is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model test twice with identical user-style prompt: baseline skill vs degraded skill section removed; assert degraded run violates behavioral expectation and fails while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in interaction/order between model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser. Unit on parser alone misses ordering and handoff defects. Rejected System because local filesystem artifact checks are enough; no required external OS/network behavior beyond normal local I/O. Rejected Agentic because content quality is irrelevant; any deterministic response payload can test capture-before-parse ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Use harness pipeline with deterministic model response fixture; assert raw response artifact is written first, then parser runs (including parse-failure case where artifact must still exist).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific field placement), not just keywords.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is assertion stack operating on parsed harness output objects (schema/path + role mapping), typically spanning parser/extractor/assertion modules. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this behavior tests assertion mechanics, not model decision quality. Rejected System since no real network/subprocess is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative parsed outputs through the real assertion pipeline: one with correct keywords but wrong field placement, one structurally correct; assert first fails and second passes.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that keyword-only checks miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and compares detection power against weaker keyword checks; correctness depends on real model outputs that can vary and drift. Rejected Workflow because a single generation evaluated by structural assertions is sufficient; no required multi-agent/multi-step sequence is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model smoke test on user-style prompt with structural assertions enabled and keyword-only baseline comparator; demonstrate a regression case that passes keyword check but fails structural check.
